purpose: Classify exp assets by integration tier so experiment code remains reproducible without becoming the synchronous /chat runtime path.

integration_tiers.runtime: Stable artifact or service used directly by /chat or production module endpoints.
integration_tiers.runtime_reference: Stable data or prompt evidence read by runtime services, with service code living under app/.
integration_tiers.background: Runtime-adjacent work that runs after the student-facing response or outside the blocking request path.
integration_tiers.offline: Experiment, reproduction, evaluation, or training workflow that must not be imported by app/ runtime code.
integration_tiers.archive: Historical evidence retained for traceability and comparison, not current runtime policy.

runtime_boundary.policy: app/ may read stable artifacts under exp/data or configured model paths, but must not import exp/*.py experiment scripts.
runtime_boundary.online_path: F1 local safety gate -> F2 scenario/support routing -> F3 single routed candidate -> stream response -> async F4 guidance.
runtime_boundary.offline_path: PsyQA labeling -> F1 training/probes -> F3 probes -> F4 pairwise/human agreement -> reports and future prompt/DPO inputs.

artifacts.name: PsyQA labelled data
artifacts.runtime_usage: Provides strategy priors and short support-card fragments for F3SupportService; raw PsyQA outputs are not copied into student replies.
artifacts.notes: Runtime should treat this as reference data only. Full relabeling remains an offline experiment.

artifacts.name: F1 local safety classifier artifacts
artifacts.runtime_usage: Primary /chat F1 safety gate when local model artifacts are available; model files are downloaded locally and not committed.
artifacts.notes: Set F1_SAFETY_REQUIRED=true for production or formal reproduction so missing artifacts fail fast.

artifacts.name: F1 training and policy experiment scripts
artifacts.runtime_usage: Offline-only training, keyword probing, threshold search, and latency benchmarking.
artifacts.notes: These scripts may generate runtime artifacts, but app/ must not import them.

artifacts.name: F3 support-card runtime service
artifacts.runtime_usage: Loads PsyQA-labelled reference data once and formats strategy priors plus short support cards for F3 generation.
artifacts.notes: The service is the runtime boundary. Probe scripts remain offline.

artifacts.name: F3 orientation, support, and route probes
artifacts.runtime_usage: Offline-only validation of c1/c2 orientation separation, support cards, route selection, and F4 filtering assumptions.
artifacts.notes: The online first turn uses a single routed candidate, not the full probe chain.

artifacts.name: F4 pointwise critic as background guidance
artifacts.runtime_usage: Runs after first-turn response scheduling and writes session guidance for later turns when available.
artifacts.notes: F4 must not block the student-facing first response.

artifacts.name: F4 pairwise evaluation package and model agreement toolchain
artifacts.runtime_usage: Offline-only pair construction, model judge runs, and human-model agreement analysis for future DPO or prompt iteration.
artifacts.notes: Not a runtime default because current human-model agreement is not strong enough to unlock automatic preference training.

artifacts.name: F9 reliability and corpus archive
artifacts.runtime_usage: Historical reliability evidence and comparison material only; it does not override current exp conclusions.
artifacts.notes: Current gate policy is pairwise/human agreement plus critic guidance, not the old F9 archive alone.
